message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Rooster and the Pig (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,753+ reviews; TripAdvisor 399 reviews; Restaurant Guru aggregate ~4,000 — combined 2000+ across major platforms
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — Yelp/Restaurant Guru aggregate near 4.6, TripAdvisor 4.4. Mild recent mixed signal in May 2026 reviews citing service/order errors at peak times, offset by continued strong food praise.
- Longevity: Opened April 2015; ~11 years operating under continuous ownership of restaurateur Tai Spendley
- Credentials: ["USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026 (per discovery sources themusehotelpalmsprings.com and visitpalmsprings.com) [UNVERIFIED on USA Today primary source]","TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award","Vogue feature (April 2017, Coachella dining guide)","Palm Springs Life feature (April 2015 + ongoing best-of inclusions)","Desert Sun feature (June 2015)","CV Independent 'Indy Endorsement' (April 2015)","The Infatuation review"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently for: 'best Vietnamese Palm Springs', 'best restaurants Palm Springs', 'where to dine Palm Springs', and Coachella-week dining roundups. Does NOT reliably surface for: 'date night Palm Springs' (Workshop, Il Corso, Eight4Nine dominate), 'fine dining Palm Springs' (loses to Workshop Kitchen + Bar), or generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (diluted by El Paseo and resort venues). Notably absent from several 2026 'downtown Palm Springs best restaurants' agent-summarized lists despite the USA Today recognition.
- Site structured data: Own website (roosterandthepig.com) is a brochure-style site. No schema.org Restaurant markup detected in extracted content. Hours not posted on landing page. Menu is present but not in machine-readable structured format. Address and phone are present as plain text. Press page is a static list with no article schema. Notable agent-readability gap for a restaurant of this stature.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify these claims through independent searches and fetches.

input.prompt: Look at the raw HTML source. Report: (1) Is there any JSON-LD script tag (application/ld+json) with schema.org Restaurant markup? (2) Is there any microdata (itemtype="http://schema.org/...")? (3) Are hours of operation displayed on the landing page? (4) Are address and phone displayed as plain text? (5) Is the menu present and is it in any structured/machine-readable format? (6) Anything that looks like structured data for press articles?

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Rooster and the Pig and USA Today recognition:

Rooster and the Pig at 356 S Indian Canyon Drive was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, the highest national recognition a Palm Springs restaurant has received in recent memory.

The restaurant serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with a tight, rotating menu built around seasonal ingredients. Signature dishes include shrimp and pork spring rolls, spicy chile okra, shaking beef, and caramelized butternut squash with pepitas and pineapple sauce.

Rooster and the Pig fills its reservation book 1-2 weeks out on weekends during peak season (November through April). However, it's worth noting that the restaurant only operates 4 days per week and does not take reservations.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs:

## Yelp and Other Review Ratings

The Yelp listing for Rooster and the Pig shows it has received 1753 reviews as of May 2026. While the specific Yelp star rating wasn't directly displayed in the search results, other review platforms show similarly strong ratings: based on 774 reviews, the restaurant has received a rating of 4.5 stars, and on Tripadvisor it's rated 4.4 of 5 and ranked #29 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs.

## Restaurant Details

Established in 2015, chef Tai Spendley crafts a straightforward, yet progressive menu drawn from his culinary influences: French technique, Asian heritage, and bi-coastal gallivanting. The restaurant is located at 356 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, and is open Wednesday through Sunday from 5:00 pm - 9:00 pm, closed Monday and Tuesday.

## Dining Experience

Reviewers consistently praise the food quality and atmosphere, though it's not possible to book, so you just need to turn up and give your name to the host and then wait, with the waiting area basically being the car park.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Structured Data Analysis: Rooster and the Pig

## Findings:

**(1) JSON-LD Restaurant Schema:** No JSON-LD script tags with schema.org Restaurant markup detected.

**(2) Microdata:** No microdata attributes (itemtype="http://schema.org/...") present.

**(3) Hours of Operation:** Not displayed on this landing page.

**(4) Address & Phone as Plain Text:** Yes. The contact information appears as plain text: "356 S. Indian Canyon DrIVE, Palm Springs, CA 92262 760.832.6691"

**(5) Menu Presence & Format:** Yes, a menu is present but it's unstructured plain text only. Menu items are organized under category headings like "you begin," "the fresh rolls," and "best you share," but lack any machine-readable markup (no JSON, microdata, or structured vocabulary).

**(6) Structured Data for Press:** No structured data found for press articles. A "press" link exists in navigation, but the content page itself contains no article markup or metadata.

**Summary:** This website relies entirely on unstructured HTML presentation without semantic markup, making automated data extraction difficult.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best date night restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Recommendations

A downtown steakhouse offers warm lighting, cozy booths, and a patio with steaks seared to perfection paired with an impressive wine list.

Copley's is an award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine located in part of the former Cary Grant estate in the heart of Palm Springs, with upscale dining either outside on the patio with mountain views or indoors.

Il Corso in Palm Springs offers an authentic Italian dining experience, with reviews praising its beautiful ambiance and excellent food and service, making it perfect for a date night.

FARM is a traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs in the historic Plaza, with a menu comprised of traditional European dishes using locally sourced produce and artisanal meats, served at a pace where you may enjoy your evening transported to the South of France.

Melvyn's at The Ingleside Inn is known for white cloth dining and is one of the last restaurants in Greater Palm Springs to offer tableside cooking with favorites like Steak Diane, with a piano bar and lounge for drinks and dancing after dinner.

4 Saints located on the rooftop of The Kimpton Rowan in Palm Springs offers an innovative approach to fine dining with craft cocktails and wines, with the Highway 111 (a date-infused bourbon old-fashioned) as a fan favorite.

Lavender Bistro combines the finest meats and seafood with the freshest organic ingredients, offering dining indoors in a warm, cozy atmosphere or on a stunning tree-lined patio, with superb live music nightly.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Rooster and the Pig:

Rooster and the Pig was established in 2015, as a 50-seat restaurant tucked into the back corner of a small strip mall on Indian Canyon that opened in early April.

Chef/owner Tai Spendley crafted a straightforward, yet progressive menu drawn from his culinary influences: French technique, Asian heritage, and bi-coastal gallivanting. After decades working in senior-level food and beverage roles at trendy hotels and restaurants in Chicago and Palm Springs, Spendley opened his first solo concept in the spirit of small, back-alley, authentic restaurants he loves to visit when he returns home to Vietnam.

Spendley chose the name based on an "Asian Astrology book that claimed that the signs of Rooster and Pig are a force to be reckoned with when combined in work." 

The restaurant has become popular in the area, with Spendley sourcing authentic produce, herbs and traditional ingredients each week from Bolsa, known as "Little Saigon" in Orange County.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top-Rated Fine Dining Establishments

Rooster and the Pig was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, making it Palm Springs' most nationally recognized restaurant. It serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with signature dishes including shaking beef and spicy chile okra.

Workshop Kitchen & Bar holds both a James Beard Foundation design award and Michelin recommendation, making it the city's most decorated fine dining venue.

## MICHELIN Guide Restaurants

The MICHELIN Guide has reached Palm Springs, with 4 Saints, the rooftop dining room at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs, making the list. The four-course prix fixe is delivered with polished, attentive service, while the à la carte menu includes standouts like wagyu steak frites with truffle fries and seared scallops with parsnip purée.

## Other Notable Fine Dining Options

Copley's, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, made OpenTable's "100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America" List for 2024 and "Diners' Choice 2026".

Bar Issi is a top choice in Palm Springs with a coastal Italian menu featuring wood-fired pizzas with seasonal farmers' market toppings to housemade pastas and Snake River Farms wagyu ribeye.

Le Vallauris is a cornerstone of fine dining in Palm Springs, offering an elegant French cuisine experience with a menu featuring classic French dishes, expertly prepared with the finest ingredients, and is renowned for its impeccable service.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Vietnamese restaurants in Palm Springs, CA:

## Top Vietnamese Restaurants

The most highly-rated Vietnamese restaurants in Palm Springs include Rooster And The Pig, Fuzion Five, 533 Viet Fusion, La Baguette Vietnamese Sandwiches, and Pho Vu.

**533 Viet Fusion** - A top rated restaurant that features locally sourced ingredients, house made sauces and a wide range of gluten-free and vegetarian dishes. They have the largest summer roll menu in the valley.

**Fuzion Five** - Located in The Vineyard Shopping Center, Fuzion Five offers a mix of Vietnamese and Lao specialties prepared with authenticity and heart.

**Pho Vu** - A family-owned restaurant that has been serving delicious Vietnamese dishes since 2009, with a menu featuring a wide selection of pho, rice dishes, and vermicelli bowls.

These restaurants offer a variety of traditional Vietnamese dishes including pho, banh mi sandwiches, spring rolls, and vermicelli bowls.

# Rooster and the Pig - USA Today Recognition

According to the page, Rooster and the Pig received the following honor:

**Honor:** "USA Today's Restaurant of the Year"

content.text: So one source (a hotel marketing blog) claims 2026 Restaurant of the Year, but another search result indicates the actual USA Today recognition was 2025 Top 47. The discovery sources cited (themusehotelpalmsprings.com) make the 2026 claim, but I cannot find primary USA Today confirmation. This claim was already flagged as UNVERIFIED in the original assessment — confirmed unverified.

confirms: Yelp review volume 1,753+ reviews (Yelp listing confirms 'Updated May 2026 - 2072 Photos & 1753 Reviews')
TripAdvisor rating 4.4 of 5, ranked #29 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs (consistent with claim of TripAdvisor 4.4)

Site structured data assessment: Independent fetch of roosterandthepig.com confirms NO JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup, NO microdata, hours NOT on landing page, address and phone present as plain text, menu present but unstructured, press page lacks article schema — exactly as claimed
Longevity: Opened April 2015 under chef/owner Tai Spendley — multiple sources (Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine) confirm ~11 years operation under continuous Spendley ownership
Surfaces for 'best Vietnamese Palm Springs' queries (Rooster and the Pig appears as top-listed Vietnamese restaurant in search results)

Fine dining query gap: 'fine dining Palm Springs' results lead with Workshop Kitchen & Bar, 4 Saints, Copley's, Le Vallauris — Rooster appears only as a sidebar mention tied to USA Today claim, supporting the claim it 'loses to Workshop Kitchen + Bar'
Date night query gap: 'best date night Palm Springs' results dominated by Copley's, Il Corso, FARM, Melvyn's, 4 Saints, Lavender Bistro — Rooster does not surface, confirming claim
contradicts: TripAdvisor review volume claim of '399 reviews' may be stale — search snippet references review counts but exact 399 not confirmed in current pulls; Restaurantji shows 364 reviews on a different aggregator. Minor, but the '399' figure not independently corroborated.
flagged_as_unverified: USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026 — already self-flagged as unverified. Independent search found USA Today actually recognized Rooster and the Pig in its 2025 'Top 47 Restaurants in the U.S.' list, not as a 2026 'Restaurant of the Year.' The 2026 Restaurant of the Year list (39 restaurants) reporting cites Tide Tables and Oceano in Florida; no primary USA Today source confirms Rooster as 2026 winner. The 2026 framing appears to originate only in secondary marketing sources (themusehotelpalmsprings.com). HIGH RISK this credential is overstated or misattributed.
Vogue feature April 2017 Coachella dining guide — not independently surfaced in current searches
Desert Sun feature June 2015 — not independently verified
CV Independent 'Indy Endorsement' April 2015 — not independently verified
The Infatuation review — not independently verified
TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award — not surfaced in current TripAdvisor result snippet
Restaurant Guru aggregate ~4,000 / 4.6 — not independently confirmed; combined 2000+ review claim is plausible given Yelp 1,753 alone exceeds it, but cross-platform total unverified
May 2026 mild mixed-signal reviews citing service/order errors — not independently sampled
input.notes: Structured-data and longevity claims fully verified. Query-surfacing claims align with independent search behavior — Rooster surfaces for Vietnamese/best-of, not for date-night or fine-dining. HOWEVER the headline credential 'USA Today Restaurant of the Year 2026' is the load-bearing claim and it does NOT hold up on primary-source check: the actual USA Today honor appears to be the 2025 'Top 47 Restaurants in the U.S.' list, while the 2026 'Restaurants of the Year' list (39 winners) does not surface Rooster in any reporting I could find. The brief's existing [UNVERIFIED] flag is justified and should be hardened — recommend downgrading to '2025 USA Today Top 47 Restaurants' which IS supported. Visibility gap score of 7 and convertible tier remain reasonable given confirmed structured-data absence on the restaurant's own site.
